name: Xamoterol
description: Xamoterol is a β1-adrenoceptor partial agonist that has shown to improve systolic and diastolic function in studies with heart failure patients. It modulates the sympathetic control of the heart but has no agonist action on β2-adrenoceptors.

interactions.name: Cabergoline
interactions.description: Cabergoline may increase the hypertensive activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.name: Bromocriptine
interactions.description: Bromocriptine may increase the hypertensive activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.name: Methylergometrine
interactions.description: Methylergometrine may increase the hypertensive activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.description: Dihydroergotamine may increase the hypertensive activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.name: Ergotamine
interactions.description: Ergotamine may increase the hypertensive activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.name: Ergoloid mesylate
interactions.description: Ergoloid mesylate may increase the hypertensive activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.name: Ergonovine
interactions.description: Ergonovine may increase the hypertensive activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.name: Venlafaxine
interactions.description: Venlafaxine may increase the tachycardic activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.name: Duloxetine
interactions.description: Duloxetine may increase the tachycardic activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.description: Milnacipran may increase the tachycardic activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.description: Levomilnacipran may increase the tachycardic activities of Xamoterol.

interactions.name: Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine
interactions.description: Xamoterol may decrease effectiveness of Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine as a diagnostic agent.

interactions.name: Spironolactone
interactions.description: Spironolactone may decrease the vasoconstricting activities of Xamoterol.
